description: Common sponsor block layout presets — the rectangular zones reserved on a livery for primary sponsor branding. SPB uses these to suggest aspect ratios and placement bounds.

schema.name: string — human label
schema.aspect_ratio: string — typical W:H ratio of placed art
schema.background_required: boolean — does this slot need a contrasting block fill

blocks.name: Primary Hood Block
tips: Reserve the hood for the title sponsor — it is the largest single uninterrupted canvas on the car
Avoid placing across hood pin lines or shutline breaks
If the hood has a center scoop, split the logo into two halves or use a wordmark that flows around it

blocks.name: Secondary Quarter Panel Block
tips: Mid-tier sponsor placement — visible from the rear three-quarter angle
Mirror across both sides of the car
Avoid the wheel-arch curve; keep logo above the fender flare line

blocks.name: Contingency B-Pillar Block
tips: Reserved for the contingency sponsor stack
See contingency_stacks.json for the matching layout presets
Keep clear of the door handle and roof flag if applicable

blocks.name: Windshield Top Banner
tips: Mandatory series identifier zone in most stock car series
Background fill is usually the team's secondary color
Wide aspect ratio — wordmarks work better than square logos

blocks.name: Rear Spoiler / Wing Block
tips: High visibility from cars following — ideal for sponsor ID during racing footage
Mirror on the underside if the spoiler is double-sided
Curve allowance — wing camber distorts long wordmarks; pre-warp the art

blocks.name: Door Panel Associate Block
tips: Smaller associate sponsor placement adjacent to the primary number panel
Common location for fuel, tire, and oil supplier logos
Maintain at least a 20px gap from the number panel stroke

blocks.name: Front Bumper Block
tips: Visible in front-on broadcast shots and pit-lane photography
Avoid overlapping headlight cutouts
Wide horizontal — best for wordmark-style sponsors

blocks.name: Nose Block
tips: Square aspect ratio — ideal for badge-style logos
Often paired with team manufacturer mark on opposite side
Keep clear of grille and badge mounting cutouts
